# Stop Using Default Values: The Real Cost of CBAM — And a One-Time PCF Path

**Default values aren't a shortcut.** They *look* convenient, but they tend to push up costs, attract customs scrutiny, and stall your working capital. The practical alternative is to **build a once-and-reusable PCF model** and reuse it across customs, buyers, and audits.

**In this article:** default-value costs · HS code data checklist · one-time PCF path · cost model · quick checklists

---

## 1) The cost of default values

- **Systematically higher tax.** Transitional default values published by the European Commission are coarse “world-average” intensities; from **2026** the regime moves to **country-average + mark-up** defaults. If your real process is cleaner than those benchmarks, **you overpay** when certificates are due. See: **[CBAM overview](https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/carbon-border-adjustment-mechanism_en)** and **[Default values note](https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2023-12/Default%20values%20transitional%20period.pdf)**.  
- **Audit/inspection risk.** Declarants relying on estimates/defaults face **higher review and re-filing risk** as customs tighten data expectations; rework locks **working capital** and can delay shipments.  
- **Lost commercial upside.** Buyers with **SBTi** commitments want **product-level** emissions. With defaults you **get no credit** for lower-carbon SKUs, weakening pricing power and tender win-rates. See **[SBTi guidance](https://sciencebasedtargets.org/standards-and-guidance)**.  
- **Management takeaway:** *Default values are a bridge, not a strategy.* Use them only as a stop-gap while you build a specific, evidence-backed PCF.

---

## 2) HS codes and a minimal data pack

**Higher-risk HS/CN segments under CBAM today:**  
**Iron & steel** (HS **72**, parts of **73**), **Aluminium** (**76**), **Cement** (HS **2523**), **Fertilisers** (**31**), **Hydrogen** (**2804**), **Electricity** (sector). Sector pages: **[Commission overview](https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/carbon-border-adjustment-mechanism_en)** · **[Per-sector info](https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/carbon-border-adjustment-mechanism/cbam-information-sector_en)**.  
> **Note on batteries (HS 85):** not in current CBAM scope, but **high-scrutiny** for buyers/DPP-ESPR/Batteries Regulation. Treat as *priority for PCF readiness* even if not CBAM-taxed today. See **[ESPR](https://commission.europa.eu/energy-climate-change-environment/standards-tools-and-labels/products-labelling-rules-and-requirements/ecodesign-sustainable-products-regulation_en)**.

**Minimal data pack per HS/CN (cradle-to-gate):**  
- **Boundary** (modules/stages covered) & **12-month** reference window  
- **Materials/BOM** (weights, grades, scrap)  
- **Process energy** by step (electricity, fuels)  
- **Main logistics legs** (inbound key feedstocks, outbound to border)  
- **Factor provenance** (factor source, version, unit; electricity grid factor or supplier-specific)  

**Rejection traps → fixes:**  
- Boundary mistakes → show a **simple boundary diagram** and module list  
- Unit inconsistency → **unit checks** + standard **templates**  
- Missing factor sources → **cite factor source/version** right in the table; keep a **factor register**

---

## 3) A one-time PCF path you can reuse everywhere

**Inputs:** product spec / BOM / energy / logistics. If a field is missing, use **industry factors** with **clearly labeled assumptions** and uncertainty.

**80/20 coverage:** model the **top emitters first** (e.g., hot metal, calcination, electrolysis power, primary ingot, key routes); iterate the long tail later.

**Deliverables (make them reusable):**  
- **Result + method** (functional unit, system boundary, allocation)  
- **Factor table** (source, version, unit), **lineage/provenance**  
- **Data-quality rating (DQR)** and **uncertainty bands**  
- **Evidence pack** (redacted invoices/meters/screenshots)  

**Reuse targets:**  
- **Customs** (CBAM registry uploads)  
- **Buyer tenders** (supplier-specific Scope 3)  
- **Audits/assurance**  
- Easy upgrades to **DPP/ESPR**, **PEF/EPD** later. See **[PEF methods](https://eplca.jrc.ec.europa.eu/EnvironmentalFootprint.html)** · **[EN 15804 explainers](https://oneclicklca.com/en/resources/articles/en-15804-changes-epds)**.

---

## 4) Economics: default values vs. one-time modeling

**Default values behave like an *annual fee* that scales with volume.**  
- **Liability formula (simplified):** `CBAM cost ≈ Embedded tCO₂e × Certificate price`  
- From **2026**, importers purchase **CBAM certificates** linked to **EU ETS** prices (definitive period). See **[Commission CBAM FAQ](https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2023-11/CBAM%20Frequently%20Asked%20Questions_November%202023.pdf)**.  
- If defaults exceed your real intensity, you **pay the spread every year**.

**One-time PCF model ≈ upfront + light refresh.**  
- **Capex-like**: build once, then quarterly/annual refresh when **energy mix**, **yields**, or **suppliers** shift.  
- **Benefits:** lower tax, faster clearance, fewer rejections, stronger pricing/credentialing with buyers.

---

## 5) Quick checklists

### Internal prep
- **Owners:** compliance · finance · production · procurement · logistics  
- **Templates:** BOM · energy · logistics · **factor register** (source/version/unit)  
- **Quality gates:** unit checks · boundary/time consistency · assumption flags  
- **Evidence policy:** minimal set · electronic · versioned

### External deliverables
- **Human-readable:** method · boundary · result · DQR · uncertainty · assumptions  
- **Machine-readable:** CSV/JSON mapped to buyer/customs fields  
- **Audit pack:** sources · screenshots/redacted docs · lineage export · change log  
- **Verification access:** QR/link with controlled permissions

---

## What Climate Seal does (in one page)
- **Faster:** ingest **BOM/energy/logistics**, auto-map rules & factors, run **batch PCF**, and output **one-click customs/buyer** reports (CBAM-ready).  
- **Credible:** every model ships with **data-quality scoring**, **uncertainty bands**, and **evidence packs**, aligned to ISO 14067/GHG Protocol Product Standard.  
- **Reusable:** the same PCF feeds **customs**, **tenders**, **audits**, and upgrades to **DPP/ESPR**, **PEF/EPD**.  
- **Actionable:** compare materials/process/suppliers to cut emissions *and* cost.

> **Call to action:** Want a **once-and-reusable PCF** for your top SKU? **[Book a Climate Seal walkthrough](https://climate-seal.com)** — we'll build a pilot and hand you the evidence pack.

# 别再用默认值：CBAM 的真实成本与一次建模的可复用 PCF 路径

**默认值不是捷径。** 它看似省事，但往往推高成本、增加海关审查概率、拖慢清关。更务实的做法是：**一次建模、反复复用的产品碳足迹（PCF）模型**，用于报关、买方投标与审计。

**本文包含：** 默认值成本 · HS 编码数据清单 · 一次建模路径 · 成本模型 · 快速清单

---

## 1）默认值的成本

- **更高的税负。** 过渡期默认值是**全球加权平均强度**；**自 2026 年**起将转为**按出口国平均 + 加价（mark-up）**的默认值。若你的真实工艺优于该基准，**缴费会被高估**。参见 **[CBAM 总览](https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/carbon-border-adjustment-mechanism_en)** 与 **[默认值说明](https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2023-12/Default%20values%20transitional%20period.pdf)**。  
- **更高的审计/复核风险。** 依赖估算/默认值更可能触发**复核与重报**，清关延误占用**营运资金**。  
- **丢失商业红利。** 承诺 **SBTi** 的买方需要**产品级**排放；用默认值无法体现**低碳 SKU** 优势，议价与中标都吃亏（见 **[SBTi 指南](https://sciencebasedtargets.org/standards-and-guidance)**）。  
- **管理层结论：** *默认值只是临时桥，不是长期策略。* 一边短期过渡，一边尽快建立可复用的专属 PCF。

---

## 2）HS 编码与最小化数据包

**当前 CBAM 涵盖的重点 HS/CN 段：**  
**钢铁**（HS **72**，部分 **73**）、**铝**（**76**）、**水泥**（HS **2523**）、**化肥**（**31**）、**氢**（**2804**）、**电力**（部门）。部门页：**[欧委会总览](https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/carbon-border-adjustment-mechanism_en)** · **[分行业信息](https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/carbon-border-adjustment-mechanism/cbam-information-sector_en)**.  
> **关于电池（HS 85）：** 当前不在 CBAM 范围，但在**买方要求 / DPP-ESPR / 电池法规**下是**高关注**品类；建议优先做 PCF 准备。参见 **[ESPR](https://commission.europa.eu/energy-climate-change-environment/standards-tools-and-labels/products-labelling-rules-and-requirements/ecodesign-sustainable-products-regulation_en)**。

**每个 HS/CN 的“最小数据包”（从摇篮到工厂大门）：**  
- **边界**（覆盖模块/阶段）与 **12 个月**参考期  
- **材料/BOM**（重量、牌号、损耗）  
- **工序能耗**（电力、燃料）  
- **主要物流**（关键来料与出境段）  
- **因子溯源**（来源、版本、单位；电网因子或供应商专属因子）  

**常见驳回点 → 修复：**  
- 边界错误 → 提供**边界示意图**与模块清单  
- 单位混乱 → **单位校验** + 统一**模板**  
- 因子缺来源 → 在因子表中**标注来源/版本**，维护**因子台账**

---

## 3）一次建模、处处复用的 PCF 路径

**输入：** 产品规格 / BOM / 能耗 / 物流。缺字段时，用**行业因子**并**显式标注假设**与不确定性。

**80/20 覆盖：** 先建**排放大头**（如高炉铁水、分解/煅烧、电解用电、初级锭、关键路段），长尾后迭代。

**交付物（可复用）：**  
- **结果 + 方法**（功能单位、系统边界、分配规则）  
- **因子表**（来源/版本/单位）、**数据血缘/来源链**  
- **数据质量评分（DQR）**与**不确定性区间**  
- **证据包**（发票/表计/截图等脱敏材料）

**复用场景：**  
- **海关**（CBAM 注册与填报）  
- **买方投标**（供应商专属 Scope 3）  
- **审计/核证**  
- 平滑升级到 **DPP/ESPR**、**PEF/EPD**（见 **[PEF 方法](https://eplca.jrc.ec.europa.eu/EnvironmentalFootprint.html)** · **[EN 15804 解读](https://oneclicklca.com/en/resources/articles/en-15804-changes-epds)**）

---

## 4）经济账：年度“默认值成本” vs 一次性建模

**默认值 ≈ 随销量线性增长的“年费”。**  
- **简化公式：** `CBAM 成本 ≈ 嵌入 tCO₂e × 证书价格`  
- **自 2026 年**起，进口商购买与 **EU ETS** 价格挂钩的 **CBAM 证书**（进入正式期）。见 **[欧委会 CBAM FAQ](https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2023-11/CBAM%20Frequently%20Asked%20Questions_November%202023.pdf)**。  
- 若默认值高于你的真实强度，你就会**年年支付差额**。

**一次性 PCF 建模 ≈ 前期投入 + 轻量更新。**  
- 类似“**小型资本化项目**”：当 **电力结构/产能/收率/供应商** 变动时按季/年更新。  
- **收益：** 降低税负、加快通关、减少驳回、增强溢价与中标力。

---

## 5）快速清单

### 内部准备
- **责任人：** 合规 · 财务 · 生产 · 采购 · 物流  
- **模板：** BOM · 能耗 · 物流 · **因子台账**（来源/版本/单位）  
- **质量门：** 单位校验 · 边界/时间一致性 · 假设标注  
- **证据策略：** 最小集 · 电子化 · 版本化

### 对外交付
- **可读版：** 方法 · 边界 · 结果 · DQR · 不确定性 · 假设  
- **机器版：** CSV/JSON，字段映射到买方/海关  
- **审计包：** 来源 · 截图/脱敏文档 · 血缘导出 · 变更日志  
- **验证入口：** 带权限控制的 QR/链接

---

## Climate Seal 怎么帮你
- **更快：** 摄入 **BOM/能耗/物流**，自动映射规则与因子，**批量建模**并一键输出**海关/买方**报告（适配 CBAM）。  
- **更可信：** 模型自带**数据质量评分**、**不确定性区间**与**证据包**，对齐 ISO 14067 / GHG Protocol。  
- **更可复用：** 一次建模，复用到**报关、投标、审计**，并顺滑升级 **DPP/ESPR**、**PEF/EPD**。  
- **更可执行：** 材料/工艺/供应商并排对比，同时降**碳**又降**本**。

> **立即行动：** 想要一个**一次建模、到处复用**的 PCF？**[预约演示](https://climate-seal.com)**——我们用你的一个 SKU 打样并交付证据包。

articles.content:
# Product Carbon Footprint (PCF): a practical guide for teams that ship real products

> **TL;DR** — A *product carbon footprint (PCF)* totals the greenhouse-gas emissions (in CO₂e) of a **single product** across its life cycle. It’s requested by buyers, used in programs like **CBAM**, and underpins construction **EPDs**. Calculate it with activity data × emission factors following **[ISO 14067](https://www.iso.org/standard/71206.html)** and the **[GHG Protocol Product Standard](https://ghgprotocol.org/product-standard)**.

**Jump to:** [What PCF means](#what-pcf-means) · [Where it’s used](#where-pcfs-are-used) · [How to calculate](#how-a-pcf-is-calculated) · [Do you need it?](#do-you-need-a-pcf) · [Pitfalls](#common-pitfalls) · [Data checklist](#a-minimal-pcf-data-checklist) · [Climate Seal](#what-climate-seal-does) · [FAQ](#faq) · [Sources](#sources)

---

## What PCF means
A **product carbon footprint (PCF)** is the total GHG emissions associated with a *product* across defined life-cycle stages—typically raw materials, manufacturing, logistics, use, and end-of-life—reported in **CO₂e**.  
**Conceptual equation:** `PCF = Σ (activity data × emission factor)` across included stages, with clear **allocation**, **data quality**, and **uncertainty** rules per the standards.

Key rulebooks:
- **[ISO 14067](https://www.iso.org/standard/71206.html)** (built on ISO 14040/44 LCA principles)  
- **[GHG Protocol — Product Life Cycle Standard](https://ghgprotocol.org/product-standard)** (PDF: [direct link](https://ghgprotocol.org/sites/default/files/standards/Product-Life-Cycle-Accounting-Reporting-Standard_041613.pdf))

---

## Where PCFs are used
- **Customer & investor disclosure.** Buyers with **Science Based Targets (SBTi)** often require *product-level* emissions. SBTi criteria: when **Scope 3 ≥ 40%** of total emissions, targets must cover **≥ 67% of Scope 3** ([source](https://sciencebasedtargets.org/standards-and-guidance)).  
- **Regulation.** The EU’s **[Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM)](https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/carbon-border-adjustment-mechanism_en)** has a transitional reporting phase (2023–2025); from **2026**, importers surrender CBAM certificates tied to embedded emissions.  
- **Construction & EPDs.** Environmental Product Declarations are based on PCF/LCA aligned with **EN 15804** (Type III declarations under ISO 14025). Good explainers: [One Click LCA](https://oneclicklca.com/en/resources/articles/en-15804-changes-epds) · [Circular Ecology](https://circularecology.com/en-15804-a2-epd-update.html).  
- **Voluntary claims & comparisons.** Early framework **[PAS 2050](https://knowledge.bsigroup.com/products/specification-for-the-assessment-of-the-life-cycle-greenhouse-gas-emissions-of-goods-and-services)** still informs practice.

---

## How a PCF is calculated
Follow **ISO 14067** / **GHG Protocol** steps:

1. **Goal & scope.** Define the product, **functional unit** (e.g., “1 kg of resin”), **system boundary** (“cradle-to-gate” vs “cradle-to-grave”), reference year, assurance needs.  
2. **Life-cycle inventory (LCI).** Gather *primary data* (energy, fuel, yields, scrap, wastes, transport) and pair with vetted **emission factors** (materials, electricity, fuels, processes). Track **biogenic carbon** where relevant.  
3. **Allocation rules.** For multi-output processes, apply mass/energy/economic **allocation** or system expansion—document your choice.  
4. **Impact calculation.** Multiply **activity data × emission factors** for each stage; roll up to the functional unit.  
5. **Uncertainty & data quality.** Quantify representativeness and uncertainty; run sensitivity checks; consider third-party **critical review** for public claims.  
6. **Reporting.** Provide boundary diagrams, assumptions, allocation, data sources, and a reproducible calculation trail; for EPDs, follow the relevant **PCR** and verification rules.

> **PCF vs corporate inventory:** A *corporate* GHG inventory totals **Scope 1, 2, 3 emissions** for the company in a year. A **PCF** follows *one product* through its life cycle. PCFs enable apples-to-apples comparisons of designs/materials/suppliers and feed credible supplier-specific data into customers’ Scope 3 reporting.

---

## Do you need a PCF?
You likely do if **any** apply:
- You sell into the EU and your goods contain **CBAM-covered** materials (steel, aluminum, cement, fertilizers, electricity, hydrogen).  
- Your customers ask for **product-level** emissions or are **SBTi-committed**.  
- You publish **EPDs** or market “low-carbon” SKUs in construction/industrial supply chains.  
- You want claims like *“30% lower embodied carbon than last year’s model”* backed by **ISO 14067 / GHG Protocol**, not vague marketing.

If none apply, a corporate inventory may be enough *for now*—but a quick **screening PCF** on your top products usually reveals cost-saving hotspots.

---

## Common pitfalls
- **Mismatched boundaries.** Comparing cradle-to-gate with cradle-to-grave.  
- **Hidden allocation.** Changing allocation rules can flip “best” suppliers—state your rule and test sensitivity.  
- **Generic factors everywhere.** Electricity market mix, time-of-use, and supplier-specific data can be material.  
- **No audit trail.** Keep invoices, meters, calculation steps, and **uncertainty bands**—expected under ISO 14067/14040.

---

## A minimal PCF data checklist
- Product **BOM** (weights/yields) or recipe/formulation  
- **Energy** by process (meters/estimates), **fuel** use, utility contracts  
- **Process** throughput, scrap/rework, recovery  
- **Logistics** routes, modes, distances (inbound/outbound)  
- **Packaging** specs and **end-of-life** assumptions  
- Priority **supplier** contacts for high-impact inputs

---

## What Climate Seal does
*Get **credible PCFs fast**, then use them to win business and plan real reductions.*

- **Faster:** Auto-map rules & emission factors and run **batch PCF modeling** from your **BOM/energy/logistics** data—then generate **one-click buyer/customs reports** (useful for **CBAM**).  
- **Credible:** Each model includes a **0–100 data-quality score**, **uncertainty bands**, **auto-fix hints**, and **evidence packs** aligned with **ISO 14067** and the **GHG Protocol Product Standard**.  
- **Frictionless supplier data:** A **low-friction, low-cost pipeline** for **verification-grade supplier data**, plug-and-play for **SBTi** target tracking and **abatement planning**.  
- **Actionable:** Side-by-side comparisons of materials, processes, and suppliers so design, procurement, and finance can pick the **lowest-emissions** (often **lowest-cost**) option.

> **Call to action:** Want **audit-ready PCFs** without six months of spreadsheets? **[Book a Climate Seal walkthrough](https://climate-seal.com)** and we’ll build a pilot on one of your SKUs.

---

## FAQ

**Carbon emission vs carbon footprint—what’s the difference?**  
*Emissions* are the gases released (e.g., kg CO₂). A *footprint* is the **sum** of those emissions across a **boundary** (e.g., a product’s life cycle).

**What are *Scope 1, 2, 3 emissions*?**  
Scope 1: direct fuel/process emissions; Scope 2: purchased energy; Scope 3: value-chain emissions (up/downstream). See the **[GHG Protocol](https://ghgprotocol.org/)**.

**What is *Science Based Targets* (SBTi)?**  
Targets aligned with climate science and validated by SBTi; when **Scope 3 ≥ 40%**, targets must cover **≥ 67% of Scope 3** ([criteria](https://sciencebasedtargets.org/standards-and-guidance)).

**How do PCFs relate to TCFD/ISSB?**  
**[TCFD recommendations](https://www.fsb-tcfd.org/recommendations/)** (now embedded in **[IFRS S2](https://www.ifrs.org/issued-standards/ifrs-sustainability-standards-navigator/ifrs-s2-climate-related-disclosures/)**) ask for decision-useful climate metrics; product-level data supports credible transition plans and capex choices.

**Do PCFs require *carbon offsets*?**  
No—PCF is an accounting result. Offsets (if used) are reported **separately**. Focus first on **abatement** (materials, energy, logistics).

**Where can I see *product carbon footprint examples*?**  
Construction **EPDs** (EN 15804) publish module-by-module footprints; many manufacturers share BOM-based examples in buyer portals.

---

## Sources
- ISO — **Carbon footprint of products (ISO 14067:2018)**: https://www.iso.org/standard/71206.html  
- GHG Protocol — **Product Life Cycle Standard** (overview): https://ghgprotocol.org/product-standard  
- GHG Protocol — **Product Life Cycle Standard (PDF)**: https://ghgprotocol.org/sites/default/files/standards/Product-Life-Cycle-Accounting-Reporting-Standard_041613.pdf  
- Science Based Targets — **Standards & Guidance**: https://sciencebasedtargets.org/standards-and-guidance  
- European Commission — **CBAM**: https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/carbon-border-adjustment-mechanism_en  
- EN 15804 explainers: https://oneclicklca.com/en/resources/articles/en-15804-changes-epds , https://circularecology.com/en-15804-a2-epd-update.html  
- BSI — **PAS 2050**: https://knowledge.bsigroup.com/products/specification-for-the-assessment-of-the-life-cycle-greenhouse-gas-emissions-of-goods-and-services  
- TCFD — **Recommendations**: https://www.fsb-tcfd.org/recommendations/

articles.title: ISO x GHG Protocol: One playbook for carbon accounting is coming
articles.titleZh: ISO x GHG Protocol：碳核算将走向“一套标准”
articles.excerpt: ISO and GHG Protocol are aligning standards and co-developing a joint PCF rulebook, reducing duplication and audit uncertainty. Expect cleaner supplier data exchange and easier assurance.
articles.excerptZh: ISO 与 GHG Protocol 宣布对齐现有标准并联合制定 PCF 规则，减少重复与审计不确定性，提升供应商数据可比性。
articles.content:
On 9 September 2025, ISO and the Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Protocol announced a strategic partnership to harmonize existing greenhouse gas accounting standards and co-develop new ones. This is not a merger of organizations. It is a plan to deliver a dual-logo, unified portfolio that reduces duplication, conflicting interpretations, and audit uncertainty for companies and assurance providers.

What is changing

- **One aligned suite:** ISO 1406x will be aligned with the GHG Protocol Corporate, Scope 2, and Scope 3 standards under a dual-logo framework.

- **A joint Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) standard:** The partners will co-develop a PCF standard to support high-granularity value chain data that informs real decarbonization decisions.

- **An integrated technical process:** Experts from both groups will run an integrated process to deliver cohesive, globally usable standards that support policy interoperability and assurance.

Why it matters (for sustainability and business teams)

- **Fewer crosswalks:** Teams that map between ISO 1406x and GHG Protocol should see fewer re-baselines, fewer duplicate reports, and clearer audit trails as alignment lands.

- **Cleaner supplier data exchange:** A joint PCF standard is expected to clarify key fields (system boundaries, allocation choices, land use change, recycled content, biogenic carbon, primary data) and improve comparability across sectors and software.

- **Policy and market usability:** The move responds to calls from the B7 business community and aligns with the ISSB push for a global baseline, leading to more consistent, comparable carbon data for investors and regulators.

What to do this quarter

1) **Stay the course and document assumptions:** Keep reporting to your current framework(s). Record organizational/operational boundaries, activity data sources, emission factor provenance, allocation rules, and Scope 2 market/location methods to make future mapping smoother.

2) **Build a field-level dual-standard data model:** Crosswalk your current fields to ISO 1406x and GHG Protocol Corporate/Scope 2/Scope 3, and leave placeholders for the upcoming joint PCF fields.

3) **Prioritize supplier primary data:** Standardize questionnaires and focus first on categories with high spend/emissions and weaker data quality.

4) **Design for auditability:** Centralize evidence (meters, invoices, MWh, t-km, allocation rationales) with metadata and lineage so third-party assurance can test once, not twice.

How Climate Seal helps (right now)

- **Automate PCFs from BOMs and supplier inputs with transparent, traceable data lineage.**

- **Map once, export twice:** Produce audit-ready outputs aligned with ISO 1406x and GHG Protocol today, positioning you for an easy transition to the dual-logo standards.

- **Accelerate supplier data collection with structured requests and built-in validation hints to improve quality at the source.**

**Ready to simplify your PCFs process?** [Book a 30‑min readiness demo](/demo?utm_source=website&utm_medium=article&utm_campaign=basic)

## Sources

- [ISO news page](https://www.iso.org/news/2025/09/iso-and-ghgp-partnership)

- [ISO media release (PDF)](https://www.iso.org/files/live/sites/isoorg/files/news/News_archive/2025/09/iso-and-ghgp-partnership/ISO%20and%20GHGP_FINAL_EN.pdf)

- [GHG Protocol press release](https://ghgprotocol.org/blog/release-iso-and-ghg-protocol-announce-strategic-partnership-deliver-unified-global-standards)

- [PR Newswire distribution](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/iso-and-ghg-protocol-announce-strategic-partnership-to-deliver-unified-global-standards-for-greenhouse-gas-emissions-accounting-302549788.html)

- [ESG Today coverage](https://www.esgtoday.com/iso-ghg-protocol-unify-standards-for-measuring-and-reporting-emissions/)

- [Carbon Market Watch/ECOS commentary](https://carbonmarketwatch.org/2025/09/09/harmonisation-of-iso-and-ghg-p-standards-must-raise-the-quality-bar/)
articles.contentZh:
9 September 2025，ISO 与 GHG Protocol 正式宣布建立战略合作伙伴关系，计划协调现有温室气体核算标准组合，并共同制定新的测量与报告标准。这不是机构合并，而是一次对方法学与治理流程的深度对齐，目标是推出带有双标识的统一标准组合，减少企业在多套规则之间来回切换所造成的重复工作与审计不确定性。

到底在改变什么

- **统一的标准组合：**ISO 1406x 系列将与 GHG Protocol 的 Corporate、Scope 2、Scope 3 标准对齐，并以双标识 形式发布。

- **联合的产品碳足迹标准（PCF）：**双方将共同开发新的 PCF 标准，以支持价值链高颗粒度数据的采集与使用，帮助企业做出更有效的减碳决策。

- **一体化技术流程：**来自两方的专家将通过综合的技术与治理流程，持续交付连贯、可核查、可与政策衔接的标准。

为什么重要（给业务与可持续团队的直白版）

- **更少的交叉对照：**过去需要在 ISO 1406x 与 GHG Protocol 之间做映射与复算。对齐后，有望减少重基线与重复披露，审计口径更清晰。

- **供应商数据更可比：**联合的 PCF 标准有望明确系统边界、分配规则、土地利用变化、再生成分、源数据粒度等关键字段，使跨行业与软件平台的数据交换更顺畅。

- **更强的政策与市场可用性：**这一步回应了 B7 商界倡议与 ISSB 全球基线的呼吁，为投资者与监管者提供更一致、可比的碳数据。

本季度建议行动

1) **保持现有披露，但把依据写清楚：**完整记录组织与运营边界、活动数据来源、因子出处、分配方法，以及 Scope 2 的市场与地理口径，方便未来平滑映射到统一标准。

2) **建一个"字段级"双标准数据模型：**将现用字段与 ISO 1406x、GHG Protocol Corporate/Scope 2/Scope 3 做一一对应，并预留即将到来的联合 PCF 字段位。

3) **抓供应链主战场：**把问卷和数据请求模板标准化，优先覆盖排放/支出占比高且数据质量薄弱的品类与工序，推动更多一手数据（primary data）。

4) **为审计准备证据链：**把仪表读数、发票、MWh、t-km、分配依据等凭证集中管理并标注元数据与来源路径，让第三方一次性可检，不必两套口径各验一遍。

Climate Seal 能帮你什么（现在就能做的）

- **从 BOM 与供应商输入自动生成 PCF，并保留透明、可追踪的数据血缘。**

- **Map once, export twice：**今天即可输出与 ISO 1406x 和 GHG Protocol 同步对齐的合规材料，为未来双标识统一标准的切换留好余地。

- **加速供应商数据收集：**用结构化请求与内置校验提示，减少来回沟通，提高源头数据质量。

**行动号召：** 预约 30 分钟演示 👉 [预约 Demo](/demo?utm_source=website&utm_medium=article&utm_campaign=basic)

## 参考资料

articles.excerpt: Putting a carbon footprint label on pack—with a clear functional unit, boundary, verification, and a QR-linked disclosure—does more than inform consumers. It aligns procurement, design, and logistics on measured hotspots, enabling year-on-year reductions and avoiding greenwashing. Here's how to do it right, and when to consider Eco-score or a broader environmental footprint label.
articles.excerptZh: 在包装上标注清晰的碳足迹标签（含功能单位、边界、第三方核查与可追溯的二维码披露），不仅是告知消费者，更能让采购、设计与物流围绕"热点"减排形成一致行动，促进年度持续改进并避免漂绿。本文给出正确做法，并说明何时采用 Eco-score 或更宽泛的环境足迹标签。
articles.content:
# Why a Carbon Footprint Label Drives Real Reductions Across Your Supply Chain

## Why carbon data on pack works
Putting a **carbon footprint label** on-pack gives shoppers decision-useful numbers and pushes suppliers to cut emissions. Randomized and field experiments show environmental labels shift choices toward lower-impact foods (see references).

**Oatly** helped mainstream prominent "climate footprint" numbers (kg CO₂e per unit) and explains boundaries in plain language—a model for salience and transparency. The lesson from the UK ASA ruling on Oatly's comparative ad ("73% less CO₂e vs milk"): comparisons must be defined precisely **in** the claim, not only in a footnote.

In the EU, expectations are rising. The **Empowering Consumers Directive (EU) 2024/825** will, by 2026, ban generic, unsubstantiated green claims and prohibit "climate-neutral" product claims based on offsets. The proposed **Green Claims Directive** is paused, but enforcement under consumer-protection law continues to tighten.

---

## Carbon footprint label vs. Eco-score vs. Environmental footprint label
- A **carbon footprint label** reports **kg CO₂e per functional unit**, typically aligned to **ISO 14067** and the **GHG Protocol Product Life Cycle Standard**—focused on climate only.
- **Eco-score** aggregates **multiple environmental impacts** (GHG, land, water, resources, toxicity, etc.) into a single rating. It's broader and useful for quick comparisons, but you must document the rules behind the score.
- An **environmental footprint label** often refers to the EU's **Product Environmental Footprint (PEF)** approach (multi-impact LCA with PEF Category Rules) or to **EPDs** (ISO 14025 / EN 15804) in construction. These require strict rules and independent verification.

**Takeaway:** If you want one number on pack that tracks climate targets, lead with a **carbon footprint label**. If you need cross-impact comparability or you work in sectors like construction, consider an **Eco-score** or **environmental footprint label** (PEF/EPD) alongside—or instead.

---

## The backbone: recognized methods & programs
- **ISO 14067** (Carbon footprint of products) & **GHG Protocol Product Standard** for quantification and reporting.
- **PAS 2050** (historic, still referenced) informed many programs.
- **EPDs** (ISO 14025; **EN 15804** for construction): third-party verified, Type III declarations with product category rules (PCRs).
- **EU PEF** (Product Environmental Footprint): harmonized, multi-impact LCA with sector-specific rules (PEFCRs) from the European Commission's JRC.

---

## How to label without greenwashing: a practical playbook
1) **Write a precise claim**  
   Print the number **and** the unit: "**0.48 kg CO₂e per 1 L, cradle-to-retail**". State **boundaries** and **functional unit** on pack; expand online.

2) **Use strong data and say how strong it is**  
   Prioritize **primary data** for hotspots; document secondary datasets and vintages; disclose data quality and allocation rules.

3) **Verify independently**  
   Obtain qualified third-party review (publish the statement). In construction or where EPDs are standard, follow ISO 14025/EN 15804.

4) **Time-stamp, version, and update**  
   Show a **version/date** on pack; set an **annual** update cadence or when processes change materially.

5) **Keep offsets off the product claim**  
   Under EU rules, don't present a product as "carbon neutral" via offsets. If you fund climate projects, describe them separately from the footprint figure.

6) **Be careful with comparisons**  
   Only compare like-for-like—same boundaries, unit, and category rules—and cite the reference **in** the claim (learn from ASA's Oatly ruling).

7) **Make details one scan away**  
   Use a QR to a public page with: method, boundaries, datasets (incl. versions), uncertainty, verifier, and change log. Many credible programs and pilots follow this disclosure pattern.

---

## Design choices: On-pack vs. online
**On pack (simple):**  
> *Climate footprint: 0.48 kg CO₂e / 1 L (cradle→retail). Includes ingredients, manufacturing, packaging, distribution. Verified to ISO 14067. v1.2 (2025-09-21). Scan for full method & verifier statement.*

**Online (complete):** method (ISO 14067/GHG Protocol), functional unit, boundaries, allocation, data sources & vintages, uncertainty, verification, update policy, and—if used—your **Eco-score** or broader **environmental footprint label** methodology (PEF/PEFCR) with links.

---

## Policy watch (EU)
- **Empowering Consumers Directive (EU) 2024/825**: bans generic green claims, unverified labels, and offset-based "climate-neutral" product claims by 2026. Plan copy and creative accordingly.  
- **Green Claims Directive**: talks paused mid-2025; expect stronger enforcement using existing consumer-protection law.  
- **France's labeling push** (ADEME): pilots and **Eco-score**-style schemes for food/textiles shape multi-impact disclosure and consumer expectations.

---

## Implementation checklist (for your launch team)
1) Choose your **method**: ISO 14067 + GHG Protocol Product (carbon); consider PEF/EPD if you need an **environmental footprint label**.  
2) Lock **functional unit** and **system boundary**; print both on pack.  
3) Gather **primary data** for hotspots; document secondary data & vintages.  
4) **Independent verification** (publish statement). For construction, issue an **EPD** (ISO 14025 / EN 15804).  
5) Draft a clear, specific **carbon footprint label** claim; avoid offsets language.  
6) Build the **QR disclosure** (method, datasets, uncertainty, verifier, versioning).  
7) If you add an **Eco-score** or **environmental footprint label**, cite the rules (PEFCR/PCR) and explain how it complements the carbon figure.  
8) Pre-clear marketing with counsel against EU consumer-law provisions; stress-test any comparisons.

---

## References & further reading (live links)
**Standards & methods**  
- [ISO 14067 overview](https://www.iso.org/standard/71206.html)  
- [ISO Online Browsing Platform (ISO 14067 context)](https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/)  
- [GHG Protocol Product Life Cycle Standard (PDF)](https://ghgprotocol.org/sites/default/files/standards/Product-Life-Cycle-Accounting-Reporting-Standard_041613.pdf)  
- [GHG Protocol Product Standard (landing)](https://ghgprotocol.org/product-standard)  
- ISO 14025 / EN 15804 overviews:  
  - [ISO TC207/SC7 page](https://committee.iso.org/sites/tc207sc7/home/projects/published/mitigation-standards.html)  
  - [EU PEF context (JRC)](https://eplca.jrc.ec.europa.eu/EnvironmentalFootprint.html)  
- EU Product Environmental Footprint (PEF):  
  - [JRC overview](https://eplca.jrc.ec.europa.eu/EnvironmentalFootprint.html)  
  - [Commission page](https://green-forum.ec.europa.eu/green-business/environmental-footprint-methods_en)

**EU policy / greenwashing rules**  
- [Directive (EU) 2024/825 (Empowering Consumers) — EUR-Lex](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2024/825/oj/eng)  
- [Official PDF](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/PDF/?uri=OJ%3AL_202400825)

**Programs, examples & enforcement**  
- [ASA ruling: *Oatly UK Ltd* (26 Jan 2022)](https://www.asa.org.uk/rulings/oatly-uk-ltd-g21-1096286-oatly-uk-ltd.html)  
- [Oatly "Climate footprint" explainer](https://www.oatly.com/oatly-who/sustainability-plan/climate-footprint-product-label)

**Eco-score / Environmental footprint label (France & EU pilots)**  
- [ADEME Environmental Labelling portal](https://affichage-environnemental.ademe.fr/en)  
- [France food labelling pilot — Government report to Parliament (2024)](https://affichage-environnemental.ademe.fr/sites/default/files/2024-09/Government%20Report%20to%20Parliament.pdf)  
- [Method notes (PEF-wise approach)](https://affichage-environnemental.ademe.fr/en/node/38)  
- [Eco Food Choice project](https://affichage-environnemental.ademe.fr/en/food-sector/life-eco-food-choice-project)

**Evidence that labels shift choices**  
- [JAMA Network Open RCT (restaurant selections, 2025)](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40758354/) — [open access](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12322791/)  
- [Working paper (meal kits/food service field experiment)](https://lisayzho.github.io/pdf/got_beef_with_beef.pdf)  
- [Randomized field experiment (grocery carbon info, 2024)](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10640-024-00873-y)

# 为什么碳足迹标签能在供应链中驱动真实减排

## 为什么"把数据放到包装上"有效
在包装上标注**碳足迹标签**，能为消费者提供可用信息，也能倒逼上游供应商减排。多项实验与现场研究显示，环境标签会引导消费者选择低影响产品（见参考链接）。

**Oatly** 以显著的"气候足迹"数值（每单位 kg CO₂e）与通俗解释提升了可见度与透明度。但其在英国的"与牛奶相比减少 73% CO₂e"广告被 ASA 判定为误导，教训是：只要涉及**比较**，就必须在**声明本身**明确比较边界与依据，而不是仅放在报告脚注里。

在欧盟，监管要求正不断提高：**《赋权消费者指令》(EU) 2024/825** 将于 2026 年前禁止笼统/无法证实的绿色宣称，并限制以抵消为基础的"碳中和"产品宣称。**《绿色宣称指令》**虽暂缓推进，但各国仍可依据消费者保护法加强执法。

---

## 碳足迹标签 vs. Eco-score vs. 环境足迹标签
- **碳足迹标签**：报告**每功能单位的 kg CO₂e**，通常遵循 **ISO 14067** 与 **GHG Protocol 产品生命周期核算标准**，仅聚焦气候影响。  
- **Eco-score**：将**多类环境影响**（温室气体、土地/水资源、资源消耗、毒性等）汇总为单一评分，便于快速比较，但需清晰披露评分规则。  
- **环境足迹标签**：多指欧盟 **PEF（产品环境足迹）** 的多指标 LCA 路径（配套 PEFCR 规则），或建筑领域的 **EPD**（ISO 14025 / EN 15804）。均要求严格规则与第三方核查。

**要点：** 若需用一个数值支撑企业气候目标，首先选择**碳足迹标签**；若强调跨影响可比性（如建筑、纺织等），可并用 **Eco-score** 或 **环境足迹标签**（PEF/EPD）。

---

## 方法与项目（被普遍认可）
- **ISO 14067** 与 **GHG Protocol 产品标准**：用于量化与报告产品碳足迹。  
- **PAS 2050**：历史上重要的规范，仍被广泛引用。  
- **EPD**（ISO 14025；**EN 15804** 用于建筑）：第三方核查的 III 型环境声明，基于 LCA 与 PCR 规则。  
- **EU PEF**：欧委会 JRC 制定的多指标 LCA 方法与行业 PEFCR 细则。

---

## 如何避免"漂绿"：实操清单
1) **精确撰写声明**  
   数字与单位并列："**0.48 kg CO₂e / 1 L（从摇篮到零售）**"，并在包装上标注**边界**与**功能单位**，详情在线披露。
2) **数据要硬，并说明"硬到什么程度"**  
   对热点环节优先采集**一手数据**；说明二手数据库与版本、数据质量与分摊规则。
3) **第三方核查**  
   取得独立机构评审并公开核查声明；建筑等领域遵循 ISO 14025/EN 15804。
4) **时间戳与版本管理**  
   包装上展示**版本/日期**；设定**年度**更新或重大变更即更新。
5) **抵消不进入产品声明**  
   欧盟规则限制以抵消支撑"碳中和"产品宣称。若有气候项目，请与产品足迹数值**分开**沟通。
6) **谨慎比较**  
   仅在**相同边界、单位与规则**下比较；在声明中标注参考依据（Oatly 个案为反面教材）。
7) **二维码一键到"完整披露"**  
   在线页包含：方法、边界、数据库与版本、不确定性、核查方与变更日志。许多可信项目与试点均采用此模式。

---

## 版式建议：包装 vs. 在线
**包装（简要）：**  
> *气候足迹：0.48 kg CO₂e / 1 L（摇篮→零售）。包含：原料、生产、包装、配送。ISO 14067 核查。v1.2（2025-09-21）。扫码查看方法与核查声明。*

**在线（完整）：** 方法（ISO 14067/GHG Protocol）、功能单位、边界、分摊、数据来源与版本、不确定性、核查、更新策略；若采用 **Eco-score** 或**环境足迹标签**（PEF/PEFCR），需说明与碳数字的互补关系并提供链接。

---

## 参考链接
**标准与方法**  
- [ISO 14067 概览](https://www.iso.org/standard/71206.html)

articles.content:
# Digital Product Passport: Linking Your Carbon Footprint Label to EU ESPR Compliance

**What's changing:** The EU's **Ecodesign for Sustainable Products Regulation (ESPR)** is now law and establishes the legal framework for **Digital Product Passports (DPPs)** across many product groups. The first mandatory DPP is the **battery passport** under the EU **Battery Regulation (EU) 2023/1542**, required from **2027** for specified batteries. Textiles and other groups will follow via **Delegated Acts**.  
– ESPR text: [Regulation (EU) 2024/1781](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1781/oj/eng)  
– Commission overview (ESPR & DPP concept): [European Commission ESPR page](https://commission.europa.eu/energy-climate-change-environment/standards-tools-and-labels/products-labelling-rules-and-requirements/ecodesign-sustainable-products-regulation_en)  
– Battery passport timeline: CEPS analysis (first legal DPP by **2027**): [CEPS in-depth brief (PDF)](https://circulareconomy.europa.eu/platform/sites/default/files/2024-03/1qp5rxiZ-CEPS-InDepthAnalysis-2024-05_Implementing-the-EU-digital-battery-passport.pdf) and industry guides ([Acquis](https://www.acquiscompliance.com/blog/eu-battery-passport-regulation-compliance-industry/))  
– Textiles roadmap (study & industry timeline): EP Research Service study ([PDF](https://www.europarl.europa.eu/RegData/etudes/STUD/2024/757808/EPRS_STU%282024%29757808_EN.pdf)); Sigma Technology summary ([2025 timeline](https://sigmatechnology.com/articles/understanding-the-eu-digital-product-passport-for-textiles/))

---

## Why your **carbon footprint label** should be the DPP front door
A good carbon label already includes a **functional unit**, **system boundary**, **verification**, and a **QR** to a public disclosure. That QR can (and should) resolve to your **DPP landing page**, so the same scan works for:  
- **Consumer info** (e.g., "0.48 kg CO₂e per 1 L, cradle→retail")  
- **Regulatory fields** (DPP data objects) required by ESPR Delegated Acts  
- **Proof for auditors & market surveillance** (verifier statements, data lineage)

Pairing your carbon label → DPP reduces duplication, aligns with **ISO 14067** / **GHG Protocol Product Standard**, and prepares you for **PEF-based** or **environmental footprint label** expansions.

---

## DPP building blocks (what to implement now)

### 1) Unique identifiers & data carriers
DPPs are linked via a **persistent product unique identifier** on a **data carrier** (QR, Data Matrix, NFC, UHF RFID) that points to a **look-up mechanism**. Expect four core identifiers across implementations: **product**, **economic operator**, **facility**, and **(often) batch/lot**.  
– Architecture & look-up: UNECE/EC slides ([PDF](https://unece.org/sites/default/files/2024-11/7Nov_pm_9TFAI_3a_API_1_EC_Auricchio%5B1%5D.pdf))  
– Carrier options & implementation notes (GS1): [GS1 paper (2025)](https://gs1.eu/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Implementation-Considerations-for-Unique-DPP-Product-Identifiers_GS1.pdf)  
– Practical primer on carriers: [Digital-link QR/NFC overview](https://digital-link-qr-code.com/el/product-transparency-digital-product-passport)

**Tip:** Use **GS1 Digital Link** in your QR so one code can resolve **consumer**, **DPP**, and **B2B** endpoints cleanly via context parameters.

### 2) Data model & access
ESPR sets the framework; **Delegated Acts** define **what fields** each category must expose. Common elements you can prepare now:  
- **Product profile:** identifiers, materials & substances (including SVHC flags), repairability/spares, firmware/updates (for electronics)  
- **Environmental performance:** **carbon footprint** (method, boundary, datasets, verification), **durability**, **recycled content**, **reusability**, **energy performance** where relevant  
- **Supply-chain actors:** economic operator & facility IDs; due diligence references  
- **Lifecycle events:** manufacturing date range, major refurbishment, end-of-life routing

Access will be **role-based** ("need-to-know") with some public and some restricted fields. Keep your QR landing public, but gate B2B/authority data.  
– Commission overview of DPP concept: [ESPR page](https://commission.europa.eu/energy-climate-change-environment/standards-tools-and-labels/products-labelling-rules-and-requirements/ecodesign-sustainable-products-regulation_en)

### 3) Logistics & updates
Battery passport goes live first in **2027**; textiles and others will follow on a **2027–2030** cadence as acts finalize. Design for **versioning** and **update cadences** (at least annual or upon material changes).  
– Batteries 2027 legal start: [CEPS](https://circulareconomy.europa.eu/platform/sites/default/files/2024-03/1qp5rxiZ-CEPS-InDepthAnalysis-2024-05_Implementing-the-EU-digital-battery-passport.pdf); [Acquis guide](https://www.acquiscompliance.com/blog/eu-battery-passport-regulation-compliance-industry/)  
– Textile milestone summary: [Sigma Technology](https://sigmatechnology.com/articles/understanding-the-eu-digital-product-passport-for-textiles/)

---

## How it connects to **Eco-score** and **environmental footprint labels**
- Your **carbon footprint label** (ISO 14067 / GHG Product Standard) becomes the climate pillar of the DPP.  
- An **Eco-score** or **environmental footprint label** (PEF/PEFCR; EPDs in construction) can be referenced as **additional multi-impact indicators** inside the DPP so you don't overload the pack.  
- Keep **comparability rules** (PCR/PEFCR) in the DPP metadata and link the **verification statements**.

**Policy fit:** ESPR + DPP complements the **Empowering Consumers Directive (EU 2024/825)**, which restricts vague claims and offset-based "neutral" language. Use the DPP to publish methods & verification and keep claims specific.  
– Empowering Consumers text: [EUR-Lex](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2024/825/oj/eng)

---

## Minimal viable DPP plan (you can start this week)
1. **Choose IDs & carriers:** Assign GS1 Global Model Number/GTIN + **GS1 Digital Link** QR for consumer/DPP endpoints; map facility IDs.  
2. **Map your data:** Carbon method (ISO 14067 / GHG Product), materials, repairability, recycled content, due-diligence refs; tag each field as *public* vs *restricted*.  
3. **Stand up the landing:** Public DPP page that your **carbon label QR** resolves to; include **footprint number**, **functional unit**, **boundary**, **datasets & vintage**, **verifier**, **version/date**, and a link to **auditor statement**.  
4. **Authorize roles:** Implement role-based access (suppliers, auditors, authorities).  
5. **Versioning & cadence:** Semantic version (e.g., v1.2), update at least annually or on process change (>10%).  
6. **Category readiness:** If you make **batteries** or battery-containing goods, prioritize battery passport fields and formats for **2027**.

---

## Where **Climate Seal** fits (available now)

*Climate Seal* is your AI agent for **Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) accounting and assurance**—focused on what's production-ready **today**:

- **Accounting engine:** Guides ISO 14067 / GHG Product–aligned scoping (functional units, boundaries), allocation rules, and data-quality scoring; can align to **PEF/PEFCR** where required.  
- **Data ingestion & supplier outreach:** Collects primary data from plants/ERPs and coordinates structured requests to Tier 1–3 suppliers; tracks coverage and response status.  
- **Assurance workflow:** Maintains an auditable trail (datasets, vintage, transformations) and assembles evidence packs (methods, calculations, change logs) for third-party review.

*Result:* a single pipeline from **PCF → verified claim → QR disclosure → DPP fields**, cutting duplication and lowering greenwashing risk.

---

## References & further reading
- **ESPR (legal text):** [Regulation (EU) 2024/1781](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1781/oj/eng)  
- **Commission explainer (ESPR & DPP):** [EC ESPR page](https://commission.europa.eu/energy-climate-change-environment/standards-tools-and-labels/products-labelling-rules-and-requirements/ecodesign-sustainable-products-regulation_en)  
- **Battery passport (first mandatory DPP, from 2027):** [CEPS analysis (PDF)](https://circulareconomy.europa.eu/platform/sites/default/files/2024-03/1qp5rxiZ-CEPS-InDepthAnalysis-2024-05_Implementing-the-EU-digital-battery-passport.pdf); [Regulation (EU) 2023/1542 industry guide](https://www.acquiscompliance.com/blog/eu-battery-passport-regulation-compliance-industry/)  
- **Textiles perspective:** [EP Research Service study (PDF)](https://www.europarl.europa.eu/RegData/etudes/STUD/2024/757808/EPRS_STU%282024%29757808_EN.pdf); [Sigma Technology timeline](https://sigmatechnology.com/articles/understanding-the-eu-digital-product-passport-for-textiles/)  
- **DPP architecture & identifiers:** [UNECE/EC slides on DPP design (PDF)](https://unece.org/sites/default/files/2024-11/7Nov_pm_9TFAI_3a_API_1_EC_Auricchio%5B1%5D.pdf); [GS1 implementation notes (PDF)](https://gs1.eu/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Implementation-Considerations-for-Unique-DPP-Product-Identifiers_GS1.pdf)  
- **Carriers (QR/NFC/RFID) primer:** [Digital-link QR/NFC overview](https://digital-link-qr-code.com/el/product-transparency-digital-product-passport)
articles.contentZh:
# 数字产品护照（DPP）：把碳足迹标签与欧盟ESPR合规真正连起来

**变化点：** 欧盟 **ESPR（可持续产品生态设计条例）** 已生效，为多类产品建立 **数字产品护照（DPP）** 框架。首个强制性DPP是 **电池护照**（依据 **《电池条例》(EU) 2023/1542**），**2027年**起适用于特定电池；纺织等品类将通过**授权法案（Delegated Acts）**分批落地。  
– 条例文本： [Regulation (EU) 2024/1781](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1781/oj/eng)  
– 委员会概览（ESPR与DPP）：[欧委会ESPR页面](https://commission.europa.eu/energy-climate-change-environment/standards-tools-and-labels/products-labelling-rules-and-requirements/ecodesign-sustainable-products-regulation_en)  
– 电池护照时间表（**2027**启用）：CEPS分析 [PDF](https://circulareconomy.europa.eu/platform/sites/default/files/2024-03/1qp5rxiZ-CEPS-InDepthAnalysis-2024-05_Implementing-the-EU-digital-battery-passport.pdf)；行业指南（[Acquis](https://www.acquiscompliance.com/blog/eu-battery-passport-regulation-compliance-industry/)）

---

## 为什么让**碳足迹标签**成为DPP入口
一个合格的碳足迹标签本就包含 **功能单位**、**系统边界**、**第三方核查** 与 **二维码披露**。让这个二维码直达 **DPP落地页**，即可一次满足：  
- **消费者信息**（例："0.48 kg CO₂e / 1 L，摇篮→零售"）  
- **法规字段**（ESPR 授权法案要求的DPP数据对象）  
- **审计与监管**（核查声明与数据溯源）

这样既符合 **ISO 14067** / **GHG Protocol 产品标准**，也便于未来扩展 **PEF 多指标**或**环境足迹标签**。

---

## DPP 实施要点（现在就能动手）

### 1) 唯一标识与数据载体
DPP 通过**持久化的产品唯一标识**与**数据载体**（QR、Data Matrix、NFC、UHF RFID）建立「查找机制」链接。常见核心ID包括：**产品**、**经营者**、**设施**（以及**批次**）。  
– 架构与查找机制：UNECE/EC 幻灯 [PDF](https://unece.org/sites/default/files/2024-11/7Nov_pm_9TFAI_3a_API_1_EC_Auricchio%5B1%5D.pdf)  
– 载体与实施建议（GS1）：[GS1 技术说明 (2025)](https://gs1.eu/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Implementation-Considerations-for-Unique-DPP-Product-Identifiers_GS1.pdf)  
– 载体入门： [Digital-link QR/NFC 概览](https://digital-link-qr-code.com/el/product-transparency-digital-product-passport)

**提示：** 采用 **GS1 Digital Link**，同一个二维码即可按上下文路由至 **消费者页**、**DPP页** 与 **B2B接口**。

### 2) 数据模型与访问
ESPR 提供框架；**授权法案**细化各品类**必填字段**。可提前准备的共性内容：  
- **产品信息**：标识、材料与物质（含高关注物质标记）、可维修性/备件、固件更新（电子类）  
- **环境绩效**：**碳足迹**（方法/边界/数据库/核查）、**耐用性**、**再生成分**、**可重复使用**、**能源性能**  
- **供应链主体**：经营者与设施ID；尽调/合规引用  
- **生命周期事件**：生产日期范围、再制造/翻新、回收去向  
访问将按**"需知即得"**进行分级，一部分公开，一部分受限。保持二维码落地页公开，同时对B2B/监管数据做权限控制。  
– DPP概念与原则： [欧委会ESPR页面](https://commission.europa.eu/energy-climate-change-environment/standards-tools-and-labels/products-labelling-rules-and-requirements/ecodesign-sustainable-products-regulation_en)

### 3) 物流与更新
电池护照首先于 **2027** 启用；纺织等将在 **2027–2030** 期间随法案推进落地。务必设计 **版本管理** 与 **更新节奏**（至少年度或关键变更时）。  
– 电池时间表： [CEPS](https://circulareconomy.europa.eu/platform/sites/default/files/2024-03/1qp5rxiZ-CEPS-InDepthAnalysis-2024-05_Implementing-the-EU-digital-battery-passport.pdf)；[Acquis](https://www.acquiscompliance.com/blog/eu-battery-passport-regulation-compliance-industry/)

---

## 与 **Eco-score** / **环境足迹标签** 的衔接
- **碳足迹标签**（ISO 14067 / GHG 产品标准）是DPP中"气候"这一栏目的基础数据。  
- **Eco-score** 或 **环境足迹标签**（PEF/PEFCR；建筑中的 **EPD**）可作为**多指标补充**放在DPP里，避免包装信息过载。  
- 在DPP元数据中保留**可比性规则**（PCR/PEFCR）与**核查声明**链接。

**政策配套：** DPP与 **《赋权消费者指令》(EU) 2024/825** 相辅相成，后者限制含糊其辞的绿色宣称和基于抵消的"碳中和"措辞。用DPP发布方法与核查，让声明具体可证。  
– 指令文本： [EUR-Lex](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2024/825/oj/eng)

---

## 最小可行DPP方案（本周就可起步）
1. **确定标识与载体：** 采用 GTIN/GMN + **GS1 Digital Link** 二维码；映射设施ID。  
2. **梳理数据表：** 碳方法（ISO 14067 / GHG 产品）、材料、可维修与再生、尽调引用；为每个字段标注 *公开/受限*。  
3. **上线落地页：** 让**碳标签二维码**直达DPP公开页；包含 **足迹数值、功能单位、边界、数据库与版本、核查方、版本/日期** 与 **核查声明链接**。  
4. **权限与角色：** 对供应商、审计方与主管部门实施分级访问。  
5. **版本与节奏：** 采用语义版本（如 v1.2），至少年度更新或重大变更即更新。  
6. **品类优先级：** 若涉及**电池**或含电池产品，优先对齐 **2027** 年电池护照字段与格式。

---

## Climate Seal 在哪里发挥作用（当前能力）

*Climate Seal* 是用于**产品碳足迹（PCF）核算与鉴证**的 AI 代理——以下是**当前可用**的能力：

- **核算引擎：** 按 **ISO 14067 / GHG 产品标准** 引导范围设定（功能单位、系统边界）、分摊规则与数据质量评估；可在需要时对齐 **PEF/PEFCR**。  
- **数据采集与供应商协作：** 从工厂/ERP 获取**一手数据**，并向 1–3 级供应商发起结构化数据请求；跟踪覆盖率与回复进度。  
- **鉴证工作流：** 维护**可审计链路**（数据集、版本、处理过程），生成第三方核查所需的证据包（方法、计算、变更记录）。

**效果：** 打通 **PCF → 已鉴证声明 → 二维码披露 → DPP 字段** 的单一流水线，降低合规与"漂绿"风险。

---

## 参考与延伸阅读
- **ESPR（法律文本）：** [Regulation (EU) 2024/1781](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1781/oj/eng)  
- **欧委会解读（ESPR & DPP）：** [ESPR 页面](https://commission.europa.eu/energy-climate-change-environment/standards-tools-and-labels/products-labelling-rules-and-requirements/ecodesign-sustainable-products-regulation_en)  
- **电池护照（首个强制DPP，2027起）：** [CEPS 深入分析 (PDF)](https://circulareconomy.europa.eu/platform/sites/default/files/2024-03/1qp5rxiZ-CEPS-InDepthAnalysis-2024-05_Implementing-the-EU-digital-battery-passport.pdf)；[行业指南](https://www.acquiscompliance.com/blog/eu-battery-passport-regulation-compliance-industry/)
